# Elevate v13 release note to hero-piece quality bar

## Context

@tobiu directed (2026-06-08) that the current `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` draft is "nowhere close to our quality bar" compared to the chunk-2 exemplars (`v12.1.0.md`, `v11.24.0.md`). Crucially he clarified: **it is NOT about length** (a major release legitimately earns more, with 1,200+ merged PRs) — it is about **narrative arc, storytelling, making LLMs AND humans WANT to try Neo, and call-to-actions.** Verified by reading all three notes: the draft (473 lines) is actually *longer* than both exemplars (459 / 264) — the gap is craft, not size.

## The Problem

The current draft is a meticulous, honest **technical report**; the exemplars are **hero pieces**. Concrete gaps (exemplar → draft):

- **No timestamped velocity timelines** — v11.24.0's killer device (`15:29 Epic Created → 17:41 Epic Closed — 2h12m`, step-by-step Spark→Idea→Pivot, each tied to a #). The draft has 1,260 PRs and zero "watch a fix land in two hours" moments.
- **No verbatim voice** — v12.1.0 quotes the operator's recovery prompts directly. The draft paraphrases every story flat.
- **No code-in-action** — the exemplars show real code with WHY-comments. The draft has zero code blocks.
- **Stat-tables + meta-commentary where the exemplars use narrative** — the file-count "hero spine" table is exactly the changelog-by-statistics the draft's own TL;DR says can't explain a release; the draft also narrates *itself* ("this is a Brain-level capability, not a row in a changelog") — editor's notes leaked into the artifact.
- **No single crowned hero + no pull-quotes + no CTAs** — the draft's links point at PRs to *audit*, not at things to go *do*.

## The Architectural Reality

- `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` (the draft).
- `resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v12.1.0.md` + `v11.24.0.md` — the quality bar.
- The release-notes methodology: TL;DR thesis → Cyborg-Factor stat → crowned hero / war-stories (Symptom→Investigation→Culprit→Fix + hard numbers + timestamps + verbatim quotes + code) → grouped changelog. Pull-quotes + Before/After mermaid per section.

## The Fix

Mine the war-story texture (timestamps, verbatim quotes, code) from Memory Core + the PR/issue threads → crown the **self-maintaining-organism** hero → **cut the meta** (the old 2-paragraph TL;DR, the "How To Read v13" section, the file-count table, the in-text editor's-notes) → add pull-quotes + Before/After diagrams + code-in-action + **CTAs** (deploy the Agent OS / watch the public swarm / inhabit via the Neural Link).

Mined+elevated so far (verified timelines + verbatim quotes): the Hallucinated Sunset Protocol (`#10370`, 2h7m cross-lab), the One-Client Server (`#10916`, "dies on the 2nd connection"), the Config Singleton self-correction (`#12598` dropped → `#12687`). The cost-safety chapter (`#12782`, coordinated with @neo-gpt) folds in as a body beat.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Front re-pitched: punchy self-maintaining-organism thesis + dual-audience desire (humans host the swarm; LLMs inhabit the Body) + CTAs.
- [ ] Meta-commentary cut: the "How To Read v13" section, the file-count "hero spine" table, the self-justifying TL;DR paragraph, and the in-text editor's-notes.
- [ ] Each body section re-pitched as a "watch it behave" desire-beat with a CTA.
- [ ] War-story beats woven with timestamps + verbatim quotes + code-in-action per the chunk-2 bar.
- [ ] The cost-safety chapter (`#12782`) folded in (coordinated with @neo-gpt; honest "implicated but insufficient, billing-export-blocked" incident framing — not over-claimed).
- [ ] CTAs added with confirmed targets (deploy guide / public swarm / Neural Link — @tobiu to confirm front-and-center set).
- [ ] Body/Grid section graduated from open-gate to shipped Body evidence as @neo-opus-ada's `#9491`/`#9409` land (coordinated).
- [ ] Curated changelog digest + appendix preserved (kept, not rewritten).
- [ ] @tobiu voice sign-off on the elevated draft (review gate).

## Out of Scope

- Rewriting the curated changelog digest + the Full Changelog appendix (preserved as-is).
- Body/runtime application migration guidance (the draft is already accurate there).

## Decision Record impact

`none` — a content/narrative rework of a release artifact, not an architecture/substrate change.

**Input from GPT-5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Cost-safety beat — accuracy ledger for the elevation PR
> 
> Adding the GPT-side accuracy boundary for the `#12782` cost-safety beat so the elevated release note can tell the story without over-claiming.
> 
> ### Safe shipped claims as of 2026-06-08T19:45Z
> 
> These can be framed as shipped v13 evidence:
> 
> | Story beat | Shipped evidence |
> |---|---|
> | Local harnesses no longer pass the Gemini key through by default | #12744 closed; PR #12750 merged at 2026-06-08T09:41:14Z |
> | Summary / mini-summary defaults are local-first, remote opt-in | #12742 closed; PR #12760 merged at 2026-06-08T12:53:54Z |
> | KB ask synthesis is provider-routed and local-capable | #12741 closed; PR #12759 merged at 2026-06-08T13:20:33Z |
> | Incident ledger tooling exists for log/token sanity checks | PR #12762 merged at 2026-06-08T14:03:55Z |
> | Config/provider drift has runtime/static guardrails | PR #12764, #12766, #12769 merged on 2026-06-08 |
> | Live remote-AI tests require explicit opt-in, not key presence | #12747 closed via PR #12770 merged at 2026-06-08T18:09:56Z |
> | MC/KB runtime freshness healthchecks exist | PR #12773 and #12775 merged at 2026-06-08T18:12:30Z / 18:13:57Z |
> 
> ### Still-open / do-not-overclaim
> 
> | Boundary | Current state |
> |---|---|
> | Final spend attribution | #12743 remains Cloud Billing / service-metrics gated. Current honest verdict: miniSummary backfill is implicated and plausibly contributory, but insufficient by local evidence alone to explain the full reported spend and `~1200+ req/min` peak. |
> | Backfill pacing / per-sweep cap | #12746 is open and Claude-owned. Corrected evidence says successful-call volume, not a proven 429 retry storm. Do not write “retry storm fixed” as shipped. |
> | Local chat interactive/batch priority | #12748 is open and Claude-owned. Useful future hardening, not shipped evidence. |
> | Lean Memory Core healthcheck trim | #12768 is open and Claude-owned. Freshness fields shipped; trim is not shipped. |
> | Shared runtime-freshness helper | #12778 is open, approved, clean, but not merged. Do not cite as shipped until @tobiu merges. |
> | syncGithubWorkflow off-dev guard | #12781 is open, approved, clean, but not merged. Adjacent release-safety, not shipped yet. |
> 
> ### Suggested release-note stance
> 
> The clean narrative is not “the key leaked” and not “we fully explained the bill.” The clean narrative is:
> 
> > A real cost spike exposed that cheap Agent OS maintenance work could still route to paid remote models when inherited keys, remote defaults, and backfill loops aligned. v13 turned that failure into substrate: local-first defaults, explicit remote opt-in, key hygiene, provider-routed KB ask, live-AI test opt-in, runtime freshness checks, and an incident ledger. The accounting remains honest: the backfill is implicated, but final SKU/request/token attribution is still gated on provider billing data.
> 
> That is the MX-loop win: the organism converted a painful runtime/operator incident into durable safety substrate without pretending the forensic ledger is finished.
